name: SegmentedControlRoot
description: Root component for SegmentedControl. Renders a group of selectable segments with a sliding indicator. Use `SegmentedControlRootPrimitive` when you need to supply a custom indicator element. Renders a `<div>` element.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The default value of the SegmentedControl. This is used when you want the SegmentedControl to manage its own state. It should be the value of one of the SegmentedControl.Item components.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Callback fired when the value of the SegmentedControl changes. It receives the new value and the original change event details from the underlying RadioGroup component. This is useful for handling side effects when the user selects a different segment.

props.name: value
props.description: The controlled value of the SegmentedControl. This should be used in conjunction with `onValueChange` to update the value when the user interacts with the SegmentedControl.

props.name: indicatorElement
props.description: A Custom element for SegmentedControl.IndicatorPrimitive. If not provided, the default SegmentedControl.IndicatorPrimitive will be rendered.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the component should ignore user interaction.

props.name: form
props.description:
Identifies the form that owns the radio inputs.
Useful when the radio group is rendered outside the form.

props.name: inputRef
props.description: A ref to access the hidden input element.

props.name: name
props.description: Identifies the field when a form is submitted.

props.name: readOnly
props.description: Whether the user should be unable to select a different radio button in the group.

props.name: required
props.description: Whether the user must choose a value before submitting a form.

props.name: style
props.description:
Style applied to the element, or a function that
returns a style object based on the component's state.

props.name: render
props.description:
Allows you to replace the component's HTML element
with a different tag, or compose it with another component.

Accepts a `ReactElement` or a function that returns the element to render.
